summary: The documentation for requests.Session.request(verify=...) says it defaults to True, but omitting verify defers to the session's verify value. Passing verify=True can therefore behave differently from leaving verify unspecified, including resetting the CA bundle path.
steps_to_reproduce: Import certifi, requests, ssl, and os.
Verify that session.verify is a directory with os.path.isdir(session.verify).
Call session.request(url="https://example.com", method="HEAD") and observe a successful response.
Call session.request(url="https://example.com", method="HEAD", verify=True) and observe an OSError stating that the path /inaccessible/path is not a suitable TLS CA certificate bundle.
expected_behavior: Leaving verify unspecified should defer to the session's verify value, including a configured CA bundle path or verify=False. The documentation should state that the method defaults to the Session value, which defaults to True.
actual_behavior: Passing verify=True uses the /inaccessible/path certifi CA bundle path and raises OSError, while leaving verify unspecified uses the session's configured CA path and returns <Response [200]>.
affected_area: requests.Session.request() verify parameter documentation and behavior
repro_code:
import certifi,requests,ssl,os

certifi.__version__
certifi.where()
requests.__version__
session = requests.session()
session.verify = ssl.get_default_verify_paths().capath
os.path.isdir(session.verify)
session.request(url="https://example.com", method="HEAD")
session.request(url="https://example.com", method="HEAD", verify=True)
